steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The parlor is called Tía Ana's 'special province' — where she runs her little school. Yet she has 'already come into the room several times' checking whether guests have left. The question asks why she keeps checking.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It reveals that Tia Ana feels as if Salomé has betrayed her trust by allowing guests into a space she considers her own.: the passage does not indicate betrayal; her checking is practical, not emotional in that way. ✗
  • Choice B — It indicates the joy that Tia Ana feels when she uses her classroom for varying purposes.: the text does not support joy, and 'never in order' is a distraction unconnected to her motivation. ✗
  • Choice C — It characterizes the room as a place where Tia Ana can go to escape social pressures.: nothing in the passage suggests she wants to flee the situation. ✗
  • Choice D — It introduces an idea that helps explain Tia Ana's apparent eagerness for Salomé's guests to leave the space.: the 'special province' detail directly explains her repeated checking — she needs the room back. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
